title: ispace to send larger payloads to the moon on SpaceX’s Starship
summary: Japanese lunar exploration company ispace is purchasing space on SpaceX's Starship lunar lander mission to deliver larger payloads to the moon. This collaboration aims to enhance lunar delivery capabilities for future missions.

title: Is this the year for a NASA authorization bill?
summary: The article discusses the possibility of a NASA authorization bill being passed this year, with appropriators expressing hope for a return to regular order in the appropriations process.

title: Fi expands Starlink direct-to-device capability into dog-tracking
summary: Fi launched a dog tracker using T-Mobile’s Starlink-enabled T-Satellite service to provide nationwide connectivity in the U.S., even outside terrestrial networks.

title: The satellite industry’s most expensive problem isn’t in orbit
summary: AST SpaceMobile deployed three additional BlueBird satellites into low Earth orbit, aiming to build a space-based cellular broadband network with 45 to 60 satellites. The company is advancing its plans despite industry challenges.

title: Venus Aerospace raises $91 million to scale rocket engine technology
summary: Venus Aerospace has secured $91 million in funding to advance its rotating detonation rocket engine technology. The capital will be used to scale development and production of the innovative propulsion system.

title: The government’s options to address strained spaceports
summary: The May 28 explosion of a Blue Origin New Glenn rocket at Cape Canaveral’s Launch Complex 36 caused significant damage and highlighted the need for government action to address strained spaceports. The incident underscores challenges in managing and maintaining launch infrastructure amid growing space industry demands.

title: Space Force adds Relativity, Impulse Space to national security launch program
summary: The U.S. Space Force has included Relativity Space and Impulse Space in its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3 Lane 1 program, expanding competition for military space missions. This move allows newer companies to bid on critical national security launches.

title: Skyroot prepares for first orbital launch attempt
summary: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian launch startup, is preparing for its first orbital launch attempt as soon as July 12. The company plans to scale up to monthly launches following this milestone.

title: D-Orbit signs launch-services contract with ArkEdge Space
summary: D-Orbit will provide launch services using its ION Satellite Carrier for Japanese startup ArkEdge Space. The contract was announced on July 8.

title: Orbit Fab hires new CEO and raises funding to support satellite refueling business
summary: Orbit Fab has appointed a new CEO and secured additional funding to advance its satellite refueling operations. The company aims to transition from technology development to commercial services.

title: Watch a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launch for record-breaking 36th time early on July 9
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch for a record-breaking 36th time on July 9, with the event available to watch live.

title: Japanese company books 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX Starship mission to the moon
summary: Japanese company ispace has booked 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX's Starship mission to the moon. This expands ispace's lunar plans by utilizing SpaceX's megarocket.

title: Dance of death between binary stars leads to an unusual supernova
summary: A study reveals that binary star systems can lead to unusual supernovae, potentially solving a long-standing mystery about interacting supernovae. This discovery sheds light on how stars die in pairs rather than alone.

title: NASA begins funding hardware for 'Skyfall' Mars helicopter mission
summary: NASA has selected Firefly Aerospace to design and build the aeroshell for the 'Skyfall' Mars helicopter mission, part of its nuclear-powered Mars mission. The aeroshell will protect the descent stage during atmospheric entry.

title: Trump gifts Artemis astronauts an American flag from the US Capitol to plant on the moon
summary: Donald Trump gifted an American flag from the US Capitol to Artemis astronauts for a potential moon landing. The flag, previously held by one of the last astronauts to walk on the moon, symbolizes national pride for future lunar missions.

title: Shoebox-sized 'detector satellites' could sniff out a nuclear bomb in space
summary: A new study proposes using a constellation of cubesats equipped with special detectors to identify nuclear weapons hidden on satellites launched by adversary nations. These shoebox-sized 'detector satellites' could enhance space-based nuclear detection capabilities.

title: Venus takes center stage with 2 easy-to-see sky shows this July. Here's when and where to look
summary: Venus will be prominently visible in the sky this July, pairing with Regulus on July 9 and a crescent moon on July 17. This provides a great opportunity for stargazers to observe these celestial events.

title: 'This is the future of spatial intelligence': Vantor unveils stunning 3D satellite views of Earth (photos)
summary: Vantor, an American Earth-observation company, has unveiled stunning 3D satellite views of Earth. This advancement is presented as a significant step in spatial intelligence.

title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope prepares for launch | Space photo of the day for July 8, 2026
summary: NASA's Roman Space Telescope is in Florida preparing for its launch in August 2026. The telescope is undergoing final preparations ahead of its scheduled liftoff.

title: The US Space Force just got a new electromagnetic weapon to jam adversary satellites
summary: The U.S. Space Force has acquired its first publicly acknowledged offensive weapon, capable of jamming adversary satellites with electromagnetic radiation. This marks a significant step in the militarization of space capabilities.

title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
summary: Students are collaborating with NASA to study coastal erosion using Indigenous knowledge at the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation. The project aims to address rapid shoreline changes affecting the community's land and heritage.

title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of Messier 3 (M3), a massive globular cluster in the Milky Way galaxy. The cluster consists of ancient stars formed from the same gas cloud, offering insights into stellar evolution.

title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
summary: NASA transferred ownership of 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center to the Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The land, formerly known as Area 400, is now part of the largest unfragmented forest block in the region.

title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft has woken from its longest hibernation period of nearly a year in good health. It is now ready to transmit science data from the distant Kuiper Belt beyond Pluto.

title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
summary: The Artemis II crew participated in the dedication of an Apollo 14 Moon tree at NASA's Johnson Space Center. This second-generation loblolly pine tree traces its lineage to seeds flown aboard Apollo 14.

title: July 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: The July 2026 Satellite Puzzler challenges participants to identify the location of a satellite image and explain its significance. This event engages the public in space imagery analysis.

title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station have photographed several host cities for the 2026 FIFA World Cup from 250 miles above Earth. This showcases the unique perspective of space-based imagery in capturing global events.

title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover in his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, celebrating America's 250th anniversary. The event highlights the nation's historical achievements in exploration and spaceflight.

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations
summary: NASA released a draft Request for Proposals (RFP) seeking industry feedback on the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy to ensure a smooth transition of low Earth orbit activities from the ISS. The move aims to gather input from American companies for future space station development.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being developed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks another step in the region's revitalization as multiple launch providers expand their facilities.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward a planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar landers as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This marks a significant step toward establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: Northrop Grumman's Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to extend the life of NASA's space telescope. This marks the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help study the galaxy's structure and dynamics.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news headline 'The art of hadrons' suggests a creative or artistic interpretation of particle physics, likely related to the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) or hadron collider experiments. The accompanying image may depict a visual representation of hadrons or particle collisions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to inform and engage the industrial community on key thematic priorities and implementation details.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to streamline operations. This innovation is poised to enhance robotic missions and satellite servicing in the space industry.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks the resumption of routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring the durability of materials for spacecraft assembly. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section focuses on preparing materials for the harsh conditions of space.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive system, likely related to space technology or satellite components. The exact details are unclear due to the placeholder text.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: Japanese company ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, with launches planned no earlier than 2030. This initiative marks ispace's expansion into a lunar integrator service.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. This investment highlights the growing industry interest in RDRE, a more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision last week.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to low Earth orbit (LEO), including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 3:12 AM.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to support standalone replacements for the ISS by releasing a draft RFP for the Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLD) program. The move follows industry demonstrations of human habitation capabilities in low Earth orbit.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. Their technology aims to make laser communications more accessible with smaller, lighter, and scalable solutions.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article discusses the largest fundraises in the first half of 2026 within the space industry. It highlights how increased investment is benefiting various space companies and projects globally.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to strengthened space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the global space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC. The mission highlights True Anomaly's rapid response capabilities in space imaging.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, Aurora, as part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have joined the competition for U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 contracts. The field of eligible companies for these awards has now expanded to seven.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, solidifying its role in Europe's launch supply chain. The agreement is one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.

title: Iceye Makes Major Move in German With New Business Entity
summary: Iceye has established a new business entity in Germany, based in Berlin and Munich, to expand its presence in the German space market. This move highlights Iceye's strategic interest in tapping into the dynamic German space industry.

title: York Space and All.Space CEOs Detail Resilient Communications Vision as Acquisition Closes
summary: York Space Systems completed its acquisition of satcom terminal company All.Space, aiming to create a complete tactical satcom ecosystem. The CEOs of both companies highlighted the vision for resilient communications following the deal.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
summary: CesiumAstro has filed an FCC application for a 737-satellite constellation named Synchronicity to offer reconfigurable connectivity for both fixed and mobile satellite users. The company, based in Austin, Texas, specializes in software-defined satellite communications technology.

title: NATO Allies Plan Hybrid HALO Constellation
summary: Eight NATO allies are collaborating to create a hybrid military satellite constellation named HALO to enhance NATO's space resilience and military capabilities. The initiative aims to integrate national military satellites into a unified system.

title: SpaceX’s Transporter-17 Mission Launches 81 Payloads
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 81 payloads on its Transporter-17 rideshare mission aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base. The mission took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Space Systems Command Awards SES 5-Year Blanket Purchase Agreement
summary: The U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command awarded SES Space and Defense a five-year blanket purchase agreement for global Ku-band connectivity services. This contract includes managed connectivity solutions.

title: Neo Space and Hughes Deploy 3 Ground Gateways to Support IFC
summary: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems have deployed three new Hughes ground gateways in Saudi Arabia and Europe to support NSG's in-flight connectivity (IFC). The announcement marks a significant expansion in NSG's connectivity infrastructure.
